line: constraint user_favorite_merchants.user_favorite_merchants_user_id_fkey: FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
reason: Identical FK definition; the hand-written migration used Postgres's default constraint name (…_fkey) while drizzle names it …_users_id_fk. Renaming a production constraint carries deploy risk for zero behavioural benefit.

line: constraint user_favorite_merchants.user_favorite_merchants_user_id_users_id_fk: FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
reason: Pair of the …_fkey entry above — same FK, drizzle-generated name.

line: trigger pending_payouts.pending_payouts_emission_conservation: CREATE TRIGGER pending_payouts_emission_conservation BEFORE INSERT ON public.pending_payouts FOR EACH ROW WHEN (((new.kind = 'emission'::text) AND (new.state <> 'failed'::text))) EXECUTE FUNCTION assert_emission_conservation()
reason: Hardening A1/C10 emission-conservation fence (migration 0044) — drizzle's schema DSL cannot represent triggers; the hand-written migration is the source of truth. Enforces minted-net <= mirror balance at the DB boundary so no future writer path can re-open the unbacked-mint hole two cold audits flagged.

line: trigger pending_payouts.pending_payouts_mint_reentry_conservation: CREATE TRIGGER pending_payouts_mint_reentry_conservation BEFORE UPDATE OF state ON public.pending_payouts FOR EACH ROW WHEN (((old.state = 'failed'::text) AND (new.state <> 'failed'::text) AND (new.kind = ANY (ARRAY['emission'::text, 'order_cashback'::text, 'interest_mint'::text])))) EXECUTE FUNCTION assert_emission_conservation()
reason: Hardening A1/C10 re-entry conservation fence (migration 0044) — the second way a row enters the counted mint set is an admin payout-retry flipping failed → pending; without this trigger the freed headroom could be double-materialised (adversarial-review P0). Drizzle cannot represent triggers.

line: trigger pending_payouts.pending_payouts_mint_insert_conservation: CREATE TRIGGER pending_payouts_mint_insert_conservation BEFORE INSERT ON public.pending_payouts FOR EACH ROW WHEN (((new.kind = ANY (ARRAY['order_cashback'::text, 'interest_mint'::text])) AND (new.state <> 'failed'::text))) EXECUTE FUNCTION assert_emission_conservation()
reason: MNY-01-INV3 fresh-mint INSERT conservation fence (migration 0067) — the THIRD way a row enters the counted mint set is a fresh order_cashback/interest_mint INSERT. 0044's INSERT trigger WHENed only on kind='emission', trusting that the cashback/interest app writers move the mirror in the same txn; a rogue/buggy writer inserting an order_cashback/interest_mint payout without the matching mirror credit would mint unbacked LOOP undetected. Runs the SAME assert_emission_conservation() (identical INV-3 rule, same check_violation SQLSTATE 23514). Drizzle's schema DSL cannot represent triggers (A2-412/A2-703); the hand-written migration is the source of truth.

line: trigger credit_transactions.credit_transactions_immutable: CREATE TRIGGER credit_transactions_immutable BEFORE DELETE OR UPDATE ON public.credit_transactions FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION reject_credit_transactions_mutation()
reason: FT-13 ledger-immutability fence (migration 0064) — credit_transactions is the append-only financial ledger; this BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger RAISEs so no writer can mutate or erase a booked money row. Drizzle's schema DSL cannot represent triggers (A2-412/A2-703); the hand-written migration is the source of truth.

line: trigger user_credits.user_credits_no_delete: CREATE TRIGGER user_credits_no_delete BEFORE DELETE ON public.user_credits FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION reject_user_credits_delete()
reason: FT-13 balance-row DELETE guard (migration 0064) — user_credits is the mutable running-balance projection (UPDATE is allowed and untouched), but deleting a balance row would orphan its credit_transactions and drop a live liability, so DELETE is fenced. Drizzle cannot represent triggers.

line: trigger credit_transactions.credit_transactions_mirror_invariant: CREATE CONSTRAINT TRIGGER credit_transactions_mirror_invariant AFTER INSERT ON public.credit_transactions DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION assert_user_credits_mirror()
reason: DAT-01-inv1 balance/ledger mirror-invariant fence (migration 0066), ledger side — a DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED CONSTRAINT TRIGGER asserting at COMMIT that a touched (user, currency)'s user_credits.balance_minor equals its credit_transactions ledger sum (INV-1 / ADR 009). Fires on the ledger-append side (a ledger row inserted without a matching balance move). Drizzle's schema DSL cannot represent triggers (A2-412/A2-703); the hand-written migration is the source of truth.

line: trigger user_credits.user_credits_mirror_invariant: CREATE CONSTRAINT TRIGGER user_credits_mirror_invariant AFTER INSERT OR UPDATE ON public.user_credits DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION assert_user_credits_mirror()
reason: DAT-01-inv1 balance/ledger mirror-invariant fence (migration 0066), balance side — the same deferred commit-time INV-1 assertion fired on user_credits INSERT/UPDATE (a balance move with no matching ledger row). Distinct custom SQLSTATE 23M01 (integrity-constraint class, 'M01'=mirror) from FT-13's 23001 and 0044's 23514. Drizzle cannot represent triggers.

DAT-01-inv1 (migration 0066): a CONSTRAINT TRIGGER also materialises a pg_constraint row (contype='t'), which pg_get_constraintdef renders as 'TRIGGER DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED' — the constraint-side pair of the credit_transactions_mirror_invariant trigger entry above. Unlike 0044/0064's plain (non-constraint) triggers, a DEFERRABLE constraint trigger needs this second allowlist line. Drizzle's schema DSL cannot represent triggers.

DAT-01-inv1 (migration 0066): the pg_constraint pair of the user_credits_mirror_invariant CONSTRAINT TRIGGER (rendered by pg_get_constraintdef as 'TRIGGER DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED'). Drizzle cannot represent triggers.
